name: inventory

capacity.note: the ledger/editorial looks take a couple more rows than the cards or timeline looks; past six entries, escalate

density.note: one clause of body per part

description: A parallel set of related items of similar weight — one content shape, four interchangeable looks.
purpose: Use for a small register of related items where each carries similar weight. Author the content once (a bold lead + detail per item, optional trailing insight) and pick the look with a variant: the default numbered ledger, a cards grid, a horizontal timeline, or a magazine-style editorial split — no re-authoring. For more than six items, escalate to list-tabular or split across slides.

eyebrow.description: Optional kicker above the title (lifts into the masthead band under Form).

items.description: Each list item is one entry, authored as `- **Lead.** detail sentence.` — the bold lead is the entry name, the rest is its description.

insight.description: Optional trailing insight or takeaway. Renders as an accent band (ledger), a centred pull-quote (cards), a kicker above the run (timeline), or an accent-ruled sidebar (editorial).

skeleton:
<!-- _class: inventory -->

`Eyebrow`

## Slide heading.

- **First entry.** One-sentence description.
- **Second entry.** One-sentence description.
- **Third entry.** One-sentence description.
- **Fourth entry.** One-sentence description.

> Optional trailing insight.
sample:
<!-- _class: inventory -->

`Framework · Four Components`

## The system has four moving parts.

- **Signal Intake.** Weekly structured collection across conversations and market data.
- **Scoring Model.** Each signal scored on confidence, recency, and relevance.
- **Decision Log.** Every call recorded with the signals that informed it.
- **Calibration Loop.** Outcomes compared to predictions each cycle.

> Signals without decisions are just noise.

whenToUse.title: A parallel register
whenToUse.body: Two to six related items of similar weight — a framework's parts, a set of principles, the moving pieces of a system.

whenToUse.title: One content, your choice of look
whenToUse.body: Write the items once; switch the variant to re-render the same content as a ledger, cards, timeline, or editorial split — no re-authoring.

whenToUse.title: A bold lead per item
whenToUse.body: Each entry reads as a short bold name followed by a one-sentence detail. Equal density across entries keeps every look balanced.

whenToUse.title: An optional closing insight
whenToUse.body: A trailing blockquote becomes the look's accent — a band, a pull-quote, a kicker, or a sidebar.

antiPatterns.title: More than six items
antiPatterns.body: The looks lose scannability past six entries (the cards/timeline looks past four). Escalate to list-tabular or split across slides.

antiPatterns.title: Ordered steps
antiPatterns.body: If sequence carries meaning, use list-steps or list-criteria. inventory entries are parallel, of similar weight.

antiPatterns.title: Nested-bullet authoring
antiPatterns.body: inventory takes an inline bold lead (`- **Lead.** detail`), not the nested `- Title` / `  - body` shape that card-style components use.

antiPatterns.title: Lopsided density
antiPatterns.body: Equalize the prose when one entry has three sentences and the rest have one — uneven density unbalances every look.

related.name: cards-grid
related.when: you want a fixed cards grid with nested-bullet authoring

related.name: list-steps
related.when: the items carry an explicit sequence

related.name: list-tabular
related.when: more than six rows, or a meta column per row

related.name: timeline-list
related.when: a vertical dated timeline rather than a parallel register

cards.label: Cards
cards.caption: The same register as an equal grid of cards, with the insight as a centred pull-quote. Best when the items read as parallel options scanned at a glance.

timeline.label: Timeline
timeline.caption: A horizontal numbered run with connectors, the insight as a kicker above. Reads the register as a left-to-right progression.

editorial.label: Editorial
editorial.caption: A magazine split — the insight as an accent-ruled sidebar beside a ruled item column (the title rides the masthead band). For a feature-statement register.
